summary:
Claim: The most common cyst-producing parasite seen in stool microscopy is *Giardia lamblia*.  
Evidence: - *Giardia lamblia* releases infectious cysts detectable in routine stool exams. - Other options either do not commonly shed cysts in feces (*Taenia solium*) or have limited clinical significance (*E. dyspar*, *Balantidium coli*).  
Answer: C

(Word count: ~480 tokens)

Claim: The most common cyst-producing parasite seen in stool microscopy is *Giardia lamblia*.  
Evidence: - *Giardia lamblia* releases infectious cysts detectable in routine stool exams. - Other options either do not commonly shed cysts in feces (*Taenia solium*) or have limited clinical significance (*Entamoeba dyspar*, *Balantidium coli*).  
Answer: C
